rulings.Case Title: RONALD NEAL VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And To Substitute Defendant For Fictitiously Named Doe Defendant In The First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 15, 2017, line 1. PLAINTIFF RONALD NEAL'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And To Substitute Defendant For Fictitiously Named Doe Defendant In The First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file first amended complaint and to substitute defendant for fictitiously named Doe defendant in the first amended complaint is granted. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 15, 2017, line 2. DEFENDANT J-M MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's general demurrer pursuant to C.C.P. § 430.10(e) on the grounds that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71 by identifying the product(s) for which defendant is responsible. Defendant's general demurrer pursuant to C.C.P. § 430.10(f) on the grounds that the complaint is uncertain is overruled as uncertainty is not a ground for a general demurrer. Defendant's general demurrer based on the statute of limitations is overruled. On its face, the complaint does not state facts demonstrating that the action is time-barred pursuant to C.C.P. § 340.2. Defendant's request to stay discovery as related to defendant pending ruling on the demurrer is denied as moot as Mr. Samora's deposition was noticed for May 31, 2017. If Mr. Samora's deposition has not yet commenced, defendant's request to stay the deposition is granted only as related to defendant J-M Manufacturing. Defendant's request for judicial notice of Item 1 is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice of Items 2, 3 and 4 is granted to the extent the Court will take judicial notice of the existence of the documents, but not the truth of the matters asserted therein. Defendant's request for judicial notice of Item 5 is granted as it appears there is no opposition thereto. Defendant's demurrer complies with C.C.P. § 430.60 and CRC 3.1112 and 3.1320. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
